strategy: He takes +10% from Ice and Myth and resists Fire and Storm 50%. Hit with Ice or Myth.
Every 5th round he sacrifices one of his Fury minions to heal 7,000-11,000 and then hits the team with a +500% self-blade Wraith for 500. This is his big swing; try to kill him before Round 5.
Keeping minions dead reduces his sacrifice fuel, but he resummons, so the real answer is speed. Burst him down inside the first five rounds.
Traps on Khan get stolen onto a minion (even Mass Feint +75% triggers it here). Protect your Feint with Indemnity, or note the cheat cannot trigger when no minions are present.
Blade heavily, Indemnity your Feint, and land an Ice or Myth nuke before his 5th-round heal. Join on time; late arrival costs 4 pips.
cheatNotes: Joining late costs you 4 pips (Mana Burn) and summons a minion. Arrive on time.
He opens by summoning at least one Fury minion, so expect an add from turn one.
He Cleanse Wards himself right after his first summon, stripping your early traps. Re-apply after his opener rather than before.
With two or more wizards he summons an extra minion, and replaces any minion you kill. Do not expect the board to stay clear.
Every 5th round he sacrifices a minion to heal 7,000-11,000 then buffs himself, his key swing. Kill him before Round 5 or burst through it.
A negative ward on Khan makes his minion steal the trap onto itself. Protect it with Indemnity.
Even Mass Feint (+75%) triggers this trap-steal here, unlike most bosses.
It cannot trigger when no minions are out, a window to trap him safely if you clear the board.
Only one trap is swapped per cast, so heavy trap stacks partially survive.
